Halimolobos, a genus of plants in the Brassicaceae family, is a type of wild mustard or cabbage. The genus Halimolobos is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of the Halimolobos genus include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Halimolobos are tea infusions, extracts, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and digestive problems.
